title: Build Portier sync console take-home
description: Deliver the take-home frontend as a coherent operations-oriented sync console covering integrations overview, sync detail, review/conflict resolution, history/audit, live Sync Now API integration, and Dockerized local run support.
design:
Full rewrite sequence:
1. Rewrite shared shadcn primitives to eliminate the unwanted base-lyra sharpness while preserving the Graphite Ops theme.
2. Extract feature-first sync-console domain, API, and session-state modules under apps/web/src/features/sync-console.
3. Keep TanStack Router route files thin: validate params, render feature screens only.
4. Use TanStack Query only for live Sync Now mutation/cache and TanStack Form only for review resolution editing.
5. Implement overview and history on top of shared selectors.
6. Implement detail + review interactive flow on top of query + session state.
7. Add Docker/docs, then code review and verification before push.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Integrations overview, detail, review, and history flows are implemented
- [ ] Sync Now calls the provided API and surfaces loading, success, empty, and failure states honestly
- [ ] Conflict review supports explicit per-field resolution choices
- [ ] Docker build and run path works locally
- [ ] README reflects final architecture, assumptions, and run instructions

COMPLETED: Replaced stale UX docs with operations-console direction and rewrote Google Stitch prompts.
IN PROGRESS: Setting up detailed execution graph in beads before implementation.
NEXT: Start with route shell, navigation, and shared sync state model.
KEY DECISION: User narrowed scope from interactive take-home flow to static design-first pages so the repo can show UI direction before behavior.
NEXT: Build simple non-interactive screens for overview, detail, review, and history using the updated UX docs.

NEXT: Start with primitive/system rewrite and shared state contracts, then execute the feature issues to closure.
COMPLETED: Web research + oracle consultation refined the architecture.
KEY DECISION: Move to a feature-first slice under apps/web/src/features/sync-console with thin TanStack Router routes, TanStack Query for live Sync Now only, feature-owned session state for simulated workflow, and TanStack Form scoped to review resolution editing.

title: Make sync workflow truthful to the real Portier API
description: The current sync workflow does not meet the take-home API requirement end to end. The app unconditionally starts MSW in the router, so browser requests to https://portier-takehometest.onrender.com are intercepted locally. Sync preview rows originate from /api/v1/data/sync shape, but review/apply confirmation semantics are still local-only and the UI explicitly says it writes to the local database only.
design:
Investigate and fix the compliance gap across router bootstrap, API package MSW wiring, and review orchestration.
- Audit apps/web/src/router.tsx and apps/web/src/mocks/browser.ts so mock mode is explicit and can be disabled for take-home verification.
- Ensure the default submission path uses the real GET /api/v1/data/sync endpoint for Sync Now instead of an always-on local interceptor.
- Rework preview/review/confirmation data flow so the UI truthfully represents what comes from the remote preview payload versus what remains locally simulated.
- If apply must remain simulated because no backend write endpoint exists, change the interaction contract and copy so the requirement is satisfied honestly or document the exact limit in README.
- Capture the chosen demo/prod strategy in docs so reviewers can tell when they are seeing real API behavior versus scaffolding.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Sync Now in the submission/default run path hits the real https://portier-takehometest.onrender.com/api/v1/data/sync endpoint
- [ ] Mock mode is explicit and does not silently intercept production-base requests in normal review flow
- [ ] Preview, conflict review, and confirmation surfaces clearly distinguish remote API data from any local simulation
- [ ] README explains the runtime mode and any remaining backend limitations honestly
notes:
Created from 2026-03-21 audit: requirement 5 non-compliance.
COMPLETED 2026-03-21: Default runtime now keeps scaffolding mocks but does not intercept sync preview endpoint. Added explicit VITE_MOCK_API switch in packages/env/src/web.ts and router startup uses localWorker by default. README documents truthful mode and mock override.
close_reason: Sync preview path now hits real endpoint by default with explicit mock-mode switch.

In apps/web/src/features/sync-console/overview/page.tsx, integrations without an active draft collapse to up-to-date unless the backend status is error. That masks backend syncing/conflict states and makes the required Synced/Syncing/Conflict/Error indicators unreliable.
design:
Rework operator status derivation so route/UI state preserves backend truth before adding local workflow overlays.
- Audit getOperatorStatus and related selector/domain helpers.
- Preserve backend synced/syncing/conflict/error when no local draft exists.
- Define precedence rules when local draft state and remote status disagree.
- Align overview filters, badges, detail header badges, and CTA selection with the same status model.
- Fold the result into the backend-owned metadata work rather than keeping another heuristic layer.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Overview inventory and priority queue can surface synced, syncing, conflict, and error truthfully even with no draft session
- [ ] Detail header status matches the same source-of-truth rules as overview
- [ ] Filter behavior matches the displayed status taxonomy
- [ ] No backend conflict/syncing state is silently rendered as up-to-date
notes:
Created from 2026-03-21 audit: status indicators do not satisfy requirement 4.1.
COMPLETED 2026-03-21: Overview/operator status mapping no longer collapses backend syncing/conflict to up-to-date. Added backend-syncing/backend-conflict states and aligned badges/CTAs. Status labels now match Synced/Syncing/Conflict/Error vocabulary.
close_reason: Backend status truthfulness fixed across overview/detail indicator surfaces.

title: Map sync API failures to clear 4xx 500 and 502 UI states
description: The sync error experience does not match the prompt. normalizeApiError exists but is unused, review-store.ts collapses thrown fetch failures to a generic Network error, and the mock error set does not cover the required 500 path. The UI therefore fails to communicate missing configuration (4xx), internal server error (500), and provider/gateway outage (502) distinctly.
design:
Make HTTP failure handling transport-aware and product-specific.
- Inspect better-fetch error shape and normalize status + payload into the SyncFetchError domain.
- Wire review-store syncNow/apply failure paths through that normalization instead of the generic fallback.
- Add deterministic verification coverage for 400, 500, and 502 responses; if 504 is kept for one scenario, document why it is supplemental rather than replacing the required 502 state.
- Update detail/review surfaces so each failure explains whether data changed and what action the operator should take next.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] 4xx errors render as configuration/request problems with actionable copy
- [ ] 500 errors render as internal server failures with truthful no-change messaging
- [ ] 502 errors render as provider/gateway outages with retry/history guidance
- [ ] Verification covers all three required failure classes
notes:
Created from 2026-03-21 audit: requirement 5 error handling non-compliance.
COMPLETED 2026-03-21: Error normalization now maps HTTP classes distinctly (4xx config, 500 internal, 502 provider unavailable, 504 timeout supplemental). review-store already consumes normalizeThrownError. Added deterministic mock handlers for 400/500/502 payloads.
close_reason: Required sync error states now mapped and represented distinctly.

title: Implement TanStack Form composable for conflict resolution
description: Replace the current review resolution form with TanStack Form composable patterns using createFormHook, withForm, and form.Subscribe for granular reactivity.
design:
Current implementation:
- features/sync-console/review/components/review-resolution-form.tsx (108 lines)
- features/sync-console/review/forms/use-review-resolution-form.ts (45 lines)

Target implementation using TanStack Form v1 patterns:

1. Create form contexts and hook:
```typescript
// forms/resolution-form.ts
export const { fieldContext, formContext, useFieldContext, useFormContext } = 
  createFormHookContexts()

export const { useAppForm, withForm } = createFormHook({
  fieldContext,
  formContext,
  fieldComponents: { ResolutionField, NotesField },
  formComponents: { SubmitButton, FormProgress },
})
```

2. Create composable field components:
- ResolutionField: Per-conflict resolution choice (local/external/merge)
- NotesField: Optional notes textarea
- DiffField: Side-by-side diff display

3. Use withForm for sub-forms:
```typescript
export const ConflictListSection = withForm({
  fieldContext,
```

title: Fix versioning model: separate remote and local tracks with CAS apply
description:
The current versioning model conflates remote version (integration.version) with local revision (localSnapshot.localVersion). After apply, the code overwrites integration.version with local version, breaking the two-track model. Additionally, stale detection is client-side only with no compare-and-swap at the apply boundary, allowing race conditions.

Key fixes needed:
1. Keep integration.version as remote-only read-only metadata
2. Add separate localSnapshot.revision (number, not string)
3. Add compare-and-swap: send expectedRevision on apply, reject with 409 if mismatch
4. Remove the cache update that writes localVersion into integration.version
5. Use server-provided preview metadata instead of client-side bumpVersion

This is a correctness fix for the sync console domain model.

title: Mock API alignment audit: 6 gaps vs real apiContract
description: Exploration of the MSW mock layer revealed 6 structural misalignments between the mock handlers and the real apiContract. These range from schema drift to a fundamental architecture issue where /local HTTP endpoints simulate client-side storage but route through fetch(), making them production dead code that only works under MSW.
design:
Gaps identified:
1. /local endpoints are a fake boundary — should be real API endpoints (if server-authoritative) or replaced with direct client-side storage (TanStack DB / IndexedDB). Current model silently breaks in production.
2. draft/resolution endpoints (GET/PUT/DELETE /api/v1/integrations/:id/draft, /resolution) exist in handlers but have no apiContract entry — schema-invisible.
3. providerHandlers (recover, degrade, provider-status) are test-harness-only but live in same handler tree with no clear separation contract.
4. 504 error case returned by sync-handlers is unmodeled in apiContract.sync.preview.errors (only 400/500/502 listed).
5. error500Handler is exported but never included in any default handler array — dead export.
6. historyHandlers still imports mockIntegrations (static) for existence check while integrationHandlers use integrationStore (mutable) — split source of truth, history handler can't see recovered/deleted integrations.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] /local boundary decision documented and implemented (real API or client-side storage)
- [ ] draftHandlers endpoints added to apiContract or explicitly marked mock-only
- [ ] providerHandlers isolated into a clearly-named test-harness module
- [ ] apiContract.sync.preview.errors includes 504
- [ ] error500Handler wired into errorHandlers array or removed
- [ ] historyHandlers uses integrationStore not mockIntegrations

title: Redesign sync console around remote fetch + local DB workflow
description: Redesign the sync console product and implementation around the approved model: each integration behaves like an independent remote, sync preview is a fetch-style command against remote state, review/apply is a local commit into an MSW-backed local database, and history must truthfully distinguish remote-recorded events from local projected commits. This epic covers product flow, domain model, local mock APIs, UI state architecture, and cleanup of misleading wrappers so a future agent can implement the redesign coherently and prepare for wireframes.
design:
Approved direction:
- Remote backend remains read-only for now (integrations/detail/history/preview).
- Local DB is explicit product infrastructure backed by MSW in-memory state and may reset on app start.
- Fetch latest is modeled as a command/mutation at the UI boundary, not a passive query, even if transport remains GET.
- Each integration has its own draft lifecycle keyed by baseVersion; applying one remote may stale drafts for others.
- Thin feature-local -api wrappers should be removed in favor of direct @portier-sync/api usage unless a local adapter adds real behavior.
- Single history timeline must preserve provenance (remote vs local) instead of pretending local commits are remote truth.
Implementation should sequence domain truth first, then mocked local APIs/data, then state orchestration, then page UX states.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Sync product model is implemented around remote fetch + local local-DB commit semantics rather than mixed implicit state
- [ ] Explicit domain objects and provenance rules exist for remote snapshots, local snapshots, preview sessions, drafts, and audit/history entries
- [ ] Local mocked data layer is defined and usable for FE realism without pretending to be real backend persistence
- [ ] Thin feature-local -api pass-through wrappers are removed or folded into direct package imports
- [ ] UI exposes stale-draft and multi-remote sequencing honestly (A can stale B/C/D drafts)
- [ ] History rendering distinguishes remote-recorded and local-applied events clearly
- [ ] Result is implementation-ready for later wireframing and future backend apply/push support
notes:
Added wireframe guidance for multiple conflicts per record and history timeline cards
UI DETAIL LOCKED: mixed-state review is approved. A single review draft may simultaneously show ready-to-apply safe items, resolved conflicts, and pending conflicts. This behavior is part of the execution reference and should not require fresh product exploration.

MIXED-STATE REVIEW REQUIREMENT: The review screen must explicitly support mixed state in one draft: (1) conflicts needing decision, (2) resolved conflicts, and (3) ready-to-apply safe items. Recommended final desktop layout is three left-rail sections plus a right-side resolver panel and sticky summary footer. title: Write docs/ARCHITECTURE.md: folder convention + state ownership + ADRs
description:
Create docs/ARCHITECTURE.md capturing decisions from the 2026-03-20 analysis session. Canonical reference so future sessions do not re-litigate settled choices.

## Sections

### 1. Folder Layer Convention
- Rule: inside features/, layer directories get a - prefix; page/feature section dirs do not
- Examples: -api/, -domain/, -state/, -ui/ are layers; overview/, detail/, history/, review/ are page sections
- Rationale: layers and page sections are visually indistinguishable without a convention; - prefix sorts layers before sections alphabetically; no tooling conflicts
- Scope: inside features/ only; top-level app dirs (routes/, components/, app/) unaffected

### 2. State Ownership Table
| Data              | Owner           | Reason                                                      |
| integrations[]    | TanStack Query  | server data; no client mutation needed                      |
| history[]         | TanStack Query  | server data; optimistic update via queryClient.setQueryData |
| reviewBatches     | Zustand store   | mutable UI state built from sync preview fetch              |
| syncErrors        | Zustand store   | transient per-integration error state                       |
| syncingId         | Zustand store   | loading indicator                                           |
| filter/search UI  | local useState  | ephemeral, page-scoped                                      |
| form state        | TanStack Form   | field-level, form-scoped                                    |

### 3. ADR-001: Zustand over useReducer / Jotai
- Status: Accepted
- Context: SyncSessionProvider was 274-line monolith; any state change re-rendered all consumers
- Decision: Zustand + immer middleware
- Rejected: useReducer (same Context blast radius); Jotai (atom-per-entity adds complexity for Record<IntegrationId,T>)
- Consequences: narrowed hooks eliminate blast radius; immer handles nested record mutations; actions live in store not closures

### 4. ADR-002: Server state stays in TanStack Query
- Status: Accepted
- Context: provider duplicated integrations[] into useState with 40-line merge effect
- Decision: delete integrations useState; pages use useSuspenseQuery directly; applyReview uses queryClient.setQueryData for optimistic history
- Consequences: eliminates dual source of truth; simpler pages; standard React Query pattern

### 5. ADR-003: TanStack Form composable pattern (no change)
- Status: Accepted (existing)
- Context: resolution-form uses createFormHook + createFormHookContexts per v1 golden path
- Decision: keep as-is; extract shared formOptions to -forms/ layer if more forms added

### 6. Dead Code Deleted and Why
- lib/api-types.ts: superseded by @portier-sync/api; file itself contained deletion notice
- lib/sync-console-store.tsx: zero consumers; all pages used useSyncSession directly
- app/providers/sync-session-provider.tsx: pass-through wrapper, no logic
- SyncSessionProvider: replaced by Zustand store
- activePreviewId: written but never read
- packages/api/src/contract/: empty dir
- packages/ui/src/hooks/: .gitkeep placeholder only
notes:
Write after portier-sync-czc and portier-sync-3gh complete so the doc reflects implemented state, not planned state.
COMPLETED: Added docs/ARCHITECTURE.md as canonical architecture record. Document includes layer-prefix folder convention, state ownership table, ADR-001 (Zustand over useReducer/Jotai), ADR-002 (server state in TanStack Query), ADR-003 (TanStack Form retained), and dead code deletion ledger. Linked decisions to implemented beads portier-sync-3gh and portier-sync-czc.

title: State: remove dead state, useReducer refactor, narrow hooks
description:
State overhaul: delete dead code, replace monolith Context provider with Zustand store.

Phase 1 - Dead code cleanup (do first):
- Delete lib/api-types.ts (superseded by @portier-sync/api; file itself says so)
- Delete lib/sync-console-store.tsx (useSyncConsole has zero consumers; all pages use useSyncSession directly)
- Delete app/providers/sync-session-provider.tsx (pass-through wrapper, no logic)
- Remove activePreviewId useState from provider (set but never read)

Phase 3 - Zustand migration (after portier-sync-3gh folder restructure):
- bun add zustand immer in apps/web
- Create features/sync-console/-state/review-store.ts with useReviewStore (immer middleware)
  State: batches: Record<IntegrationId, ReviewBatch>, syncErrors: Partial<Record<IntegrationId, SyncFetchError>>, syncingId: IntegrationId | null
  Actions: syncNow, updateDecision, toggleSelection, approveSafeChanges, applyReview, clearError
- integrations state + 40-line merge effect → DELETE; pages use useSuspenseQuery(integrationsListQueryOptions()) directly
- historyByIntegration → use queryClient.setQueryData on history query inside applyReview (optimistic update)
- Narrowed hooks: useSyncingId, useReviewBatch(id), useSyncError(id), useReviewActions() using useShallow
- Delete SyncSessionProvider entirely after all consumers migrated
- sync-session-selectors.ts stays as pure functions; called inside Zustand selectors

Decision rationale: Zustand over useReducer because state is action-oriented with entity-keyed records (Record<IntegrationId, T>); immer handles nested updates cleanly; narrowed selectors eliminate Context re-render blast radius.
notes: COMPLETED: Removed dead state abstraction and migrated review workflow state from React Context provider to Zustand + immer store in features/sync-console/-state/review-store.ts. Deleted SyncSessionProvider and pass-through AppSyncSessionProvider wrapper. Deleted dead files lib/api-types.ts and lib/sync-console-store.tsx. Added app/query-client.ts singleton and rewired router to use it so store actions can update TanStack Query cache. Store now owns reviewBatches + syncErrors + syncingId with actions syncNow/updateReviewDecision/toggleReviewSelection/approveSafeChanges/applyReview/clearError. integrations and history now read from TanStack Query; applyReview writes optimistic history via queryClient.setQueryData(historyKeys.list). Updated detail/review/history pages and integration route to consume query data + narrowed Zustand hooks. VERIFIED: bun run check:types and bun run check:lint in apps/web both pass.

title: Migrate API layer from raw fetch to ts-rest
description: Replace the current raw fetch implementation with ts-rest client. Update all API calls to use the typed contract from packages/api.
design:
Current implementation:
- apps/web/src/features/sync-console/api/sync-preview.ts (raw fetch)
- apps/web/src/features/sync-console/api/sync-preview.query.ts (mutation wrapper)

Migration steps:
1. Install ts-rest dependencies:
   - @ts-rest/core
   - @ts-rest/react-query

2. Define contract in packages/api/src/contract/sync.ts:
   ```typescript
   export const syncContract = c.router({
     preview: {
       method: 'GET',
       path: '/api/v1/data/sync',
       query: z.object({ application_id: z.string() }),
       responses: {
         200: SyncPreviewResponseSchema,
         400: ErrorSchema,
         500: ErrorSchema,
         502: ErrorSchema,
       },
     },
   })
   ```

3. Create client in packages/api/src/client/:
   ```typescript
   export const syncClient = initQueryClient(syncContract, {
     baseUrl: 'https://portier-takehometest.onrender.com',
   })
   ```

4. Update feature hooks to use ts-rest client:
   - Replace useMutation with syncClient.preview.useQuery()
   - Type-safe params and response
   - Built-in error typing

5. Remove old api/ files after migration

Benefits:
- End-to-end type safety
- Auto-generated query keys
- Contract-first design
- Zod runtime validation

title: Replace hardcoded sync conflict heuristics with truthful conflict strategy
description: Current conflict detection is a hardcoded per-provider field allowlist in buildBatchFromApi. That makes fields with changed values appear non-conflicting without explaining the policy, and it does not model operator workflows like inspect, stage, bulk-apply, or data-driven conflict metadata. Explore and design a truthful conflict strategy driven by data/model semantics instead of UI heuristics.
design:
Designer + oracle approved direction:
- Replace fake conflict semantics with a unified staged-changes workflow.
- Every row is a changed item. Distinguish operator states: needs decision, pending, staged, applied.
- Stop using the word conflict unless backend proves one via base/common-ancestor data or explicit backend classification.
- Left pane stays list-first; every row can be staged, but items needing decision remain unstaged/disabled until the operator chooses current/incoming/custom in the detail pane.
- Bulk actions operate on staging eligibility, not fake conflict heuristics: select/stage all ready items, skip needs-decision items.
- Future true conflicts fit the same interaction shell: focus item, inspect base/current/incoming, resolve, then stage.
- Immediate work splits into two phases: (1) UI/domain truth cleanup using current payload, (2) API/schema upgrade adding backend-owned review requirement, reason codes, and base_value for future three-way merge.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Current conflict heuristic and its failure modes are documented
- [ ] Alternatives are evaluated against real sync workflows (per-item selection, bulk actions, staged apply, stale drafts)
- [ ] A recommended domain model and implementation path is captured
- [ ] Bead notes are sufficient for future implementation without re-exploration
notes:
NEW DIRECTION (brief-aligned): the staged-review model shipped in .q5b.1-.3 is still semantically too complex for the take-home brief. Current issue: client-side field allowlist auto-stages some changed rows and marks only others as requiresDecision, which creates a truth gap and confusing UI where decided items are also staged.

Approved simplification after repo audit + designer consult:
- If current_value !== new_value, require explicit operator decision.
- If values are equal, omit from review entirely.
- Remove heuristic auto-staging for changed rows.
- Replace the staged primary mental model with an inbox model: Pending Review -> Reviewed -> Apply Decisions.
- Apply is enabled only when Pending count reaches zero.
- Prefer record-scoped bulk actions (Accept All Incoming / Keep All Current) over global blind bulk apply.

This direction better matches the take-home brief's emphasis on safe, transparent, reviewable, auditable field-level conflict resolution using only current_value/new_value payload data.
close_reason: All phases complete: .1-.3 (initial cutover), .5-.8 (explicit decision model). .4 remains open for future API upgrade.

title: Cut over sync review domain from fake conflicts to staged review semantics
description: Replace the client-invented conflict boolean and its derived counters with a truthful domain model: every row is a changed item, some require a manual decision before they can be staged. This is the core model cutover that unblocks the UI rewrite without waiting for backend schema changes.
design:
Target files: apps/web/src/features/sync-console/-domain/review.ts, apps/web/src/features/sync-console/-state/review-store.ts, related selectors/types/tests if present.

Implementation direction:
- Replace `conflict: boolean` with a review/staging model that separates:
  1. whether the item needs manual decision (`reviewRequirement` or equivalent)
  2. whether the operator has made that decision (`decisionState`)
  3. whether the item is staged/selected for apply (`selected`)
- Rename domain/store counters and actions away from conflict language: e.g. `conflictCount` -> `manualDecisionCount`, `unresolvedCount` -> `undecidedCount`, `approveSafeChanges` -> `stageReadyChanges`.
- Keep current payload limits explicit: if the current mock/API still uses a heuristic to decide `reviewRequirement`, quarantine that as temporary policy and stop presenting it as conflict truth.
- Update apply validation and audit payload naming so they reflect staged items and decided items, not conflict resolution.
- Preserve stale-draft guard semantics (`baseVersion === localSnapshot.localVersion`).

Non-goals:
- Do not introduce true merge-conflict states yet.
- Do not add backend schema fields in this bead.
acceptance_criteria:

title: Replace staged-review model with explicit decision-per-difference workflow
description: Cut over the current staged-review workflow to a simpler, brief-aligned review model: every differing field requires an explicit operator choice, unchanged values stay out of review, and apply is only possible after all differences are reviewed.
design:
Product direction:
- Treat the review queue as an inbox of differences, not a staging area.
- Any row where current_value !== new_value enters Pending Review.
- Operator must choose Keep Current or Accept Incoming for every row.
- After choice, row moves to Reviewed.
- Apply uses reviewed decisions only; no row is auto-staged simply because the client heuristic thinks it is safe.
- Group primarily by Pending Review / Reviewed, optionally by record within each group.
- This supersedes the current auto-stage heuristic model introduced in q5b.1-.3 for the take-home implementation.

Key engineering consequence:
- Remove field-name allowlist as the UI decision gate for the current API shape.
- Decision requirement is derived from value difference, not provider-specific policy.
- Leave room for future backend metadata in q5b.4, but do not wait for it.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] No changed row is auto-staged by heuristic
- [ ] Every differing row requires explicit operator choice before apply
- [ ] Review UI presents Pending Review and Reviewed clearly
- [ ] Apply is disabled until Pending count is zero
- [ ] End-to-end flow remains fetch -> review -> apply -> history

title: Fix: ReviewResolutionForm retains merged-value state across focus changes
description: When an operator clicks Custom value on item A (which puts kind='merged' into TanStack Form state), then clicks a different pending item B without submitting, the merged-value textarea remains visible on item B. The choice buttons correctly show no selection (they use currentKind from item.resolution.kind which is undefined for a fresh pending item), but the <Subscribe> that gates the textarea reads from the form store's internal s.values.kind which is still 'merged' from item A.
design:
Root cause:
- ReviewResolutionForm has no key prop at its call site in page.tsx.
- key={item.id} exists on the inner <form> HTML element inside the component, which only remounts the DOM subtree, not the component instance and its hooks.
- useReviewResolutionForm creates the TanStack Form store at hook level. Without a component remount, the store keeps stale values.
- The <Subscribe selector={(s) => s.values.kind}> returns the stale "merged" value, causing the textarea to appear on any next item.

Fix:
- Add key={focusItem.id} to <ReviewResolutionForm> in review/page.tsx. This forces a full component remount on every focus change, which reinitialises the TanStack Form store with the new item defaults.
- Remove the now-redundant key={item.id} from the inner <form> element in review-resolution-form.tsx.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Custom value textarea does not appear when navigating to a fresh pending item after editing custom value on a previous item
- [ ] Switching focus between any two pending items always shows a clean un-selected form state
- [ ] Switching to a reviewed item (local/external) still shows the correct previously-decided state
- [ ] npx tsc --noEmit exits 0

2. TooltipTrigger in review/page.tsx was used without the base-ui render prop. Base-ui Trigger defaults to a <button> element, so the existing code nested <span><Button disabled> inside that button — invalid HTML. The base-vs-radix rule requires render= for all base-ui triggers.

title: /local endpoints are a production dead-end: decide real API vs client-side storage
description: queries/local.ts calls fetch('/local/integrations/:id/snapshot'), fetch('/local/integrations/:id/apply-review'), and fetch('/local/history'). These only resolve because MSW intercepts them. In production (no MSW), all three 404. The application code has a hard runtime dependency on a test artifact.
design:
DECISION (oracle-validated): Server-authoritative.

Rationale:
- CAS revision token on LocalSnapshotRecord must be shared across operators and sessions; browser-only resets on reload, making stale-draft detection meaningless outside one tab.
- AuditEntry has origin='future-push' / eventType='push-remote': a later backend job must know what was applied locally at which localRevision. Browser storage is the wrong substrate for that handoff.
- Operators view a unified timeline merging remote history + local audit entries. That is operational history, not per-browser notes.
- The existing apply-review endpoint already performs server-style CAS, revision increment, and audit append — it IS a server, just faked in MSW.

Endpoint promotion plan:
  GET  /local/integrations/:id/snapshot       -> GET  /api/v1/integrations/:id/snapshot
  PUT  /local/integrations/:id/apply-review   -> PUT  /api/v1/integrations/:id/apply-review
  GET  /local/history?integrationId=          -> GET  /api/v1/integrations/:id/audit (or unified with existing history)

Multi-operator semantics: optimistic concurrency. First apply against current revision succeeds; subsequent applies with same revision get 409. Clients must re-fetch preview before retrying.

Implementation steps:
1. Add snapshot/apply-review/audit endpoints to apiContract with schemas
2. Move MSW handlers from /local/* to /api/v1/* prefix
3. Update queries/local.ts to call new paths
4. Remove localHandlers entirely once MSW handlers cover the same contracts
5. Define LocalSnapshotSchema in packages/api/src/schema/
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] fetch('/local/...') calls eliminated from all application code
- [ ] State lives in either real API endpoints (with contract entries) or client-side storage
- [ ] No MSW-only code paths remain in queries/ or features/

Schema in schema/local.ts, contract in api-contract.ts local group, fetch-schema updated, MSW handlers rewritten to BASE_URL/api/v1 paths, queries/local.ts now uses typed $fetch calls. No /local/ paths remain. Commit: 5a10f48. Docs update tracked in portier-sync-q72.
